beats.text: Here's our system — d-u-d-t equals matrix negative-one, two, one, negative-two, times u. Initial condition: u of zero equals one, zero.
Eigenvalues first. The characteristic polynomial gives lambda-one equals zero and lambda-two equals negative three. Two eigenvectors appear: x-one at two, one and x-two at one, negative-one.
General solution: u of t equals c-one times e-to-the-zero-t times two, one — plus c-two times e-to-the-negative-three-t times one, negative-one.
Plug in t equals zero. The exponentials both collapse to one. Two equations: two-c-one plus c-two equals one, and c-one minus c-two equals zero. Solving gives c-one equals c-two equals one-third.
As t grows, the term carrying e-to-the-negative-three-t shrinks toward zero. An arrow for u of t drifts and settles at the steady-state vector two-thirds, one-third.
